Requirement Summary: Launch Chromium with isolated profile via Playwright
Support 11 primitive operations: launch, goto, wait_for, click, type, scroll, screenshot, get_text, get_html, evaluate_js, close
Auto retry logic: max 3 retries with exponential backoff
Domain allowlist enforcement; default: web.telegram.org only
Python class interface only, no external HTTP API
Configuration: web_url, channel_name, profile_path, history_days, poll_interval
One-time manual login; persist session in browser profile
Locate and open target channel by display name
Full history extraction by scrolling upward to cutoff date
Real-time monitoring loop with auto reconnect on crash
Extract: message_id, sender, text, html, media, timestamp
Screenshot fallback for DOM extraction failures
No forwarding/re-posting to any external Telegram account
Local SQLite database at configured path
messages_raw table as per schema in Section 10.1
De-duplication by (channel_name, message_id)
Structured JSON Lines log file for all events
Parsing Agent reads messages_raw where parsed=FALSE
LLM extraction to trade_signal JSON schema
Validate: instrument whitelist, direction, SL/TP sanity checks
Store all parse results in signals_parsed with status field
Notification on new valid signal (Telegram bot or email)
Auto-trigger on new messages_raw rows
Execute orders on CFD broker API for valid signals
Risk checks: max per-trade %, max positions, session hours, daily loss limit
Support market and limit/stop-entry order types
Log all orders and fills to trade_log table
DRY_RUN mode by default; live_trading flag required for live
Slippage reconciliation per trade
Reliability: >=99% uptime; auto-restart within 60s on crash
Performance: message to DB within poll_interval + 15 seconds
Maintainability: DOM selectors in external selectors.yaml
Portability: Windows 10+ and Ubuntu 22.04+ without code changes
Observability: structured JSON logs, configurable log level
Testability: mock browser and LLM; 70% unit test coverage
Browser profile dir accessible to OS user only (chmod 700)
API keys in env vars or .env only; never in source control
No network port exposed; all control is in-process Python
Domain allowlist in browser controller
Screenshots in local dir; no cloud sync or network share
No redistribution of messages to external services
Trading Agent and Extraction Agent run as separate processes
